scenario: An ops team wants to set up Prometheus / Grafana monitoring for their Self-Managed Camunda 8 cluster. They want to know which metrics are most important to monitor.
question: Which Camunda 8 metrics are typically monitored in production?

options.text: Camunda 8 components expose a rich set of Prometheus metrics covering cluster health, throughput, latency, error rates, and resource utilisation. Limiting monitoring to simple up/down status means missing early warning signs of performance degradation, accumulating incidents, exporter failures, or replication lag -- all of which could lead to undetected production problems before they become critical outages.

Teams do not need to instrument the Camunda components themselves -- they need to configure their Prometheus scraper to collect the existing metrics and build Grafana dashboards on top of them. Custom metrics are appropriate only for application-specific KPIs that the platform does not already expose.

Camunda 8 Self-Managed components expose extensive metrics through Spring Boot Actuator integration. The Prometheus metrics endpoint is available at <server>:9600/actuator/prometheus (management port, separate from the application port :8080). Exposed actuator endpoints include health checks, Prometheus metrics, loggers, usage metrics, and backup status. These metrics are ready to be scraped by a Prometheus instance and visualised in Grafana without any additional instrumentation work.

The key Prometheus metric categories to monitor in production Camunda 8 include: cluster health (broker liveness, partition health, replication lag); throughput (instances and jobs per second per task type); latency (instance duration and job activation percentiles p50/p95/p99); errors (incident counts, gRPC error rates, exporter failures); resource utilisation (CPU, memory, disk, network per component); and Connector Runtime (subscription health, polling success rates). Expose via <server>:9600/actuator/prometheus and aggregate in Grafana with alerting thresholds.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 components (Zeebe gateway, Operate, Tasklist, Identity, Connector Runtime) are Spring Boot applications exposing standard Prometheus metrics on the management port :9600 via /actuator/prometheus (separate from app port :8080 -- common Pattern #9 trap). Categories worth monitoring: Cluster health: are brokers up? Partitions healthy? Replication caught up? ES / opensearch reachable? Throughput: how many instances / jobs / messages / decisions per second? Is the system keeping up with load? Latency: instance durations? Job activation delays? Time-to-completion percentiles? Errors: are incidents accumulating? Are gRPC errors elevated? Are exporters failing? Resources: CPU / memory / disk / network usage of each component? Application-specific: Connector Runtime subscription / polling health, worker queue depths. Set alerts: incidents > N, gRPC error rate > X%, p99 latency > Y seconds, disk usage > 80%, replication lag > Z seconds. Tune to your SLAs. Use Camunda's recommended dashboards / Helm charts (where available) as starting templates.

c.text: Incorrect. Standard metrics + custom for app-specific.
